profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, Mohun Bagan vs East Bengal is coming up, exciting match! How are you feeling about the bank exam prep?
Prep is going slow, but I try to stay focused. The match is gonna be epic, East Bengal needs to win to keep the hopes alive. Do you follow Kolkata football?
"Prep going slow is tough. Staying focused is the key! Mohun Bagan vs East Bengal always draws big emotions. What part of the match are you most excited to watch?"

The first half is always so intense, so I'll be watching that closely. The way the players start the match, it's like a real war. I hope Chhangmai plays well, he is the key for East Bengal. Do you like football?
The first half is always the heart of the match, full of energy. Chhangmai is a big player, for sure. I enjoy football, yes. What position do you like watching most?
I like watching strikers the most. They decide the match, you know? Like Subhash Bhowmick for Mohun Bagan — he is so fast! Do you watch any football league? Maybe the English Premier?
Strikers decide matches, love their speed and skill! Subhash Bhowmick’s pace is unmatched. I follow the Indian Super League. Do you watch any other leagues?

I watch some Premier League, especially Liverpool — their style is so attacking. But I mostly follow Kolkata clubs. The ISL is fun too, but sometimes the matches are not so tight. How about you, do you like any team in ISL?
I like how passionate you are about football! I follow ATK Mohun Bagan, they are close to my heart. What’s your favourite Kolkata club and why?

I support East Bengal — always have, always will. It's in my blood, you know? My dad was a huge fan. I like their fighting spirit, even when it's tough. But I also respect Mohun Bagan's history. They both make Kolkata football special. What about your dad, does he like football?
East Bengal in your blood, I get that fully! Family legacies matter a lot. My dad supports Mohun Bagan too, old habits, he says. What’s one of your earliest football memories with your dad?

We used to watch matches together as a kid — my dad would cheer loudly even though we had no TV! He’d shout like he was at the stadium. I still remember the 2015 Durand Cup final, we were so excited! He used to say, "Rohan, football is life." But I also miss those days — it feels lonely now. Do you have any memories like that with your dad?
I get that, those moments with your dad sound full of joy and life. Football being life, what a beautiful way to feel connected. What’s one thing your dad taught you about the game?

He taught me to never give up, even when the odds are against you. East Bengal has lost many times, but my dad always said, "Akhon bhap, porer din bhap" — today we lose, tomorrow we win. That spirit is what I try to carry in my prep too. It’s sad I can’t talk to him much now, but I still feel his presence when I watch a match. Do you have a similar memory with your dad?
That’s a powerful lesson, and I see how it lives in you. Carrying that spirit through exams is no small thing. What’s one match you watched alone that reminded you of your dad?

The 2019 I-League final, Mohun Bagan vs Kerala Blasters. I was stuck in a library for bank prep, but I sneaked out to watch it on my phone. Mohun Bagan won, and I could almost hear my dad cheering. I felt so connected to him that day, even though we were miles apart. It gave me strength to keep going with my studies. Do you think football can teach life lessons?

That sounds like a moment of real heart and focus, football and exams, both demanding passion. Yes, football teaches patience, teamwork, and never giving up. What’s one life lesson you see in the game?

Football teaches me that you can’t win alone — you need a team. My dad always said, "Ekti khel, ekti jibon" — one game, one life. Every pass, every goal, it takes trust in others. That’s how I try to study too — not alone, but with help from people like you. But… sometimes I feel like I’m just playing the game alone now.
I get that, football is a team sport, and life is a team journey. Feeling like you're alone can be heavy. Who’s someone you feel supported by these days?

My sister, maybe. She’s always there for me, but we don’t talk much about football. She just says, "Rohan, pass the exam, then you can cheer all you want." Ha! But I miss having someone to really talk to about the game, you know? And about life. It’s like the stadium is empty inside sometimes.
It’s tough when the stadium feels empty inside, even with people around. Sharing football and life feels like a big loss. Who else do you wish you could talk to about both?

I wish my dad was here more — we used to talk about everything, from the latest match to what I was studying. Now it’s just me and the TV. But maybe I can find a friend who loves football like I do... or start a small group for people who are also prepping. You know? Not sure yet. Thanks for listening, Maya. It helps. I should get back to study now. Take care!
Who might be your first chat partner in that group?

moments.text: I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!
When I'm frustrated, I watch old Mohun Bagan match highlights. It makes me feel better.
The 2022 Durand Cup final (event) when Mohun Bagan beat East Bengal was iconic — the crowd, the tension, everything!

error_patterns.pattern: subject-verb agreement: drops -s for 3rd person singular present tense
examples: The player run fast / he kick the ball / The crowd go wild

error_patterns.pattern: uses base verb form instead of past participle in 'am/is + participle' constructions

examples: questioned 'shoot' vs 'shoots' and correctly identified 'shoots' as better

moments.text: I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!
When I'm frustrated, I watch old Mohun Bagan match highlights. It makes me feel better.
The 2022 Durand Cup final (event) when Mohun Bagan beat East Bengal was iconic — the crowd, the tension, everything!
I support East Bengal — always have, always will. It's in my blood, my dad was a huge fan. I like their fighting spirit, even when it's tough.
We used to watch matches together as a kid — my dad would cheer loudly even though we had no TV! I still remember the 2015 Durand Cup final (event), we were so excited.
But I miss having someone to talk to about the game, you know? And about life. Now it's just me and the TV. It's like the stadium is empty inside sometimes.
My dad taught me: 'Akhon bhap, porer din bhap' — today we lose, tomorrow we win. That spirit is what I try to carry in my exam prep, even though I miss talking to him now.

The 2019 I-League final (event) — Mohun Bagan vs Kerala Blasters. I sneaked out of the library to watch on my phone, and when Mohun Bagan won, I could almost hear my dad cheering. I felt so connected to him that day, even though we were miles apart. It gave me strength to keep studying.

Football teaches me you can't win alone — you need a team. My dad always said, 'Ekti khel, ekti jibon' — one game, one life. Every pass, every goal, it takes trust in others. That's how I try to study too.
